name: Sam Jones
abstract: Samuel Jones (November 12, 1924 – December 15, 1981) was an American jazz double bassist, cellist, and composer.

concerts.label: Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Hayes
about_quotes.evidence: Cannonball and Nat and Sam and myself, we became such good friends, and we were so close musically, and we were on stage and off, I mean the best of friends.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louis Hayes interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Sam Jones played cello, one tune a night. And on this tune a night – I was the bass player for this one tune a night. ... While we're on the Sam Jones subject, you subbed again for him with Monk.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Walter Booker and Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: We had a band, I was with one of Cannonball Adderley’s first bands, him and Nat, and Junior Mance and Sam Jones and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Walter Booker and Jimmy Cobb interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 30, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: The band was what I just named: him and Nat, Sam Jones, Junior Mance, and me. We made an album called Sharpshooters.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: But we did do a recording with Sam Jones, Cannon and Nat and Nat Perrilliat, James Black and me
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: we had a great bass player in Dizzy’s band at the time, we had Sam Jones
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]
